description: S19 (mark conversation read) at the state layer. Intends: echo peer mirrors a sent message back as INBOUND → unread increments → mark_read (open/activate) drops it to 0. STATUS: nonBlocking/FLAKY. As of 2026-05-30 (#27) the unread is read from the PATH-INDEPENDENT FfiChatService.getC2CUnreadCount (persistence + lastView barrier), so the under-count when the binary-replacement hook persists the inbound first is fixed at the test surface — but the live echo + DHT timing still makes the `unread > 0` observation race-prone across runs, so one pass is not proof and this stays nonBlocking, NOT a stable gate. #18: text carries a per-run {{nonce}} so stragglers can't be miscounted.
